message:
feat(raptorq): make SIMD intrinsics opt-in for stable Rust compatibility

Remove `simd-intrinsics` from default features in Cargo.toml so the
crate compiles on stable Rust without requiring `#![feature(portable_simd)]`.

The nightly-only `portable_simd` feature gate in lib.rs is now conditional
on `cfg(feature = "simd-intrinsics")`. In src/raptorq/gf256.rs, all SIMD
types (`Simd`, `NibbleTables`), the `use std::simd::prelude::*` import,
and the wide multiplication/addmul kernels (`mul_with_table_wide`,
`addmul_with_table_wide`) are gated behind `#[cfg(feature = "simd-intrinsics")]`.

Scalar fallback implementations are provided for the non-SIMD path:
- `NibbleTables` becomes a unit struct with a no-op constructor
- `mul_with_table_wide` falls back to per-byte table lookups
- `addmul_with_table_wide` falls back to per-byte XOR with table lookup
- The exhaustive `nibble_tables_exhaustive` test is gated to SIMD-only

This allows CI and downstream consumers to build on stable while
retaining full SIMD performance when the feature is explicitly enabled.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -31,7 +31,7 @@ include = [
 ]
 
 [features]
-default = ["test-internals", "simd-intrinsics"]
+default = ["test-internals"]
 # Enable internal test helpers for integration tests.
 # This exposes private APIs like Cx::new() and is NOT for production use.
 test-internals = ["dep:tracing", "dep:tracing-subscriber", "dep:visibility"]
